---
title: "Pacvec_tick_infection"
output: html_document
date: "2026-07-10"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

#Create github repository for this project

usethis::use_github()

#load data as of 06-10-2026
#2026 tick data counts are current but no infection data yet
#2024 to 2025 data with infection data but some samples need confirmation still
tick<-read.csv("data/raw_data/pacvec_tick_23_to_26.csv", header=T)

#change tick infection to factor 
tick$Infection <- as.factor(tick$Infection)
tick$year_f<-as.factor(tick$year)
head(tick)
str(tick)
colnames(tick)

sub_tick<-tick[!is.na(tick$Infection),]

#subsample data to just be IPAC nymphs from 2023-2025

tick_sub<- sub_tick %>%
  filter(species == "IPAC", stage == "N")

tick_sub23<- sub_tick %>%
  filter(species == "IPAC", year == "2023")
  
tick_sub24<- sub_tick %>%
  filter(species == "IPAC", year == "2024")
  
tick_sub25<- sub_tick %>%
  filter(species == "IPAC", year == "2025")
  
sub_tick<- bind_rows(tick_sub23, tick_sub24, tick_sub25)
str(sub_tick)

#load tidymodels package which includes parsnip and yardstick packages
install.packages("tidymodels")
library(tidymodels)

install.packages("xgboost")
library(xgboost)

#Create training and test set

##############################
## create train/test split ##
##############################

#install training package
install.packages("rsample")
library(rsample)

#set seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(123)

#create split with 80% for training
tick_split<-initial_split(sub_tick, prop=0.8)

#extract training and test sets

tick_train <-training(tick_split)
tick_test <- testing(tick_split)

dim(tick_test) 
dim(tick_train) #confirm split as requested

######Specify and Train models #####
##Building a basic decision tree ###
# Build the specification 
#notes 7/10/26 basic decision tree model assessment is not working. 
model_spec <- decision_tree() %>%
  set_mode("classification") %>%
  set_engine("rpart")

# Fit to the data
model_fit <- model_spec %>%
  fit(formula = Infection~ treatment_specific+year+site+drag_num,
      data = tick_train)

model_fit

#Predict new values with the test dataset 
# Train the model
tick_model <- model_spec %>%
  fit(formula = Infection~ treatment_specific+year+site+drag_num,
      data = tick_train)

# Predict new data
predictions <- predict(tick_model,
                       new_data = tick_test) %>%
  # Add the test set
  bind_cols(tick_test) #adding test set to the predictions tibble

predictions

#Assessing model performance with MAE or RMSE
# Predict using the training set
in_sample_predictions <- predict(tick_model,
                                 new_data=tick_train)

# Calculate the vector of absolute differences
abs_diffs <- (abs(in_sample_predictions$.pred_class - tick_train$Infection))

# Calculate the mean absolute error
1 / length(abs_diffs) * sum(abs_diffs)

#Predict ratings on test set and add true grades
test_enriched <- predict(tick_model, new_data = tick_test) %>%
    bind_cols(tick_test)
    
# Compute the mean absolute error using one single function
##not working
mae(test_enriched,
    estimate=.pred_class,
    truth = Infection)

#Root mean square error penalizes larger predictions
# Calculate the squared differences
# Calculate the squared differences
squared_diffs <- (test_enriched$.pred - test_enriched$final_grade)^ 2

# Compute the RMSE using the formula
rmse_manual <- sqrt(1 / length(squared_diffs) * sum(squared_diffs))

# Compute the RMSE using a function
rmse_auto <- rmse(test_enriched,
                 estimate=.pred_class,
                 truth = Infection)

# Print both errors
rmse_manual
rmse_auto
#####################################
#Specify a boosted regression tree
boost_spec<-boost_tree() %>%
  set_mode("classification") %>%
  set_engine("xgboost")

#Train model on training set and analyze tick infection dataset to predict "infection" yes/no against treatment site year etc. 
boost_model<-fit(boost_spec, formula = Infection~treatment_specific+year+site+drag_num, data=tick_train)

boost_model

#evaluate the ensemble with resampling and folds using auc as the metrics
set.seed(99)

# Create CV folds
folds <- vfold_cv(tick_train, v=5)

# Fit and evaluate models for all folds
cv_results <- fit_resamples(boost_spec,
                            Infection~ treatment_specific+year+site+drag_num,
                            resamples = folds,
                            metrics=metric_set(roc_auc))

# Collect cross-validated metrics
collect_metrics(cv_results)

#full model and compare to single classifier
set.seed(100)

# Specify, fit, predict, and combine with training data
predictions <- boost_tree() %>%
  set_mode("classification") %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% 
  fit(Infection ~ treatment_specific + year + site + drag_num, data = tick_train) %>%
  predict(new_data = tick_train, type = "prob") %>% 
  bind_cols(tick_train)

# Calculate AUC
#having issues here. go backa nd remove unecesary columns like identifyer name, date, etc,
roc_auc(predictions, 
        truth = Infection)

roc_auc_vec(predictions, truth = Infection, estimate = .pred_Yes)
